2024-10.notes: Final Oct 2024 PMI = 57.5 (rose from Sep 2024 8-month low). Data collected Oct 8-23, 2024; released Nov 4, 2024. Source: Business Standard citing S&P Global/HSBC final release.

2024-11.notes: Final Nov 2024 PMI = 56.5 ('PMI slips to 56.5, but remains firmly in expansion'). Softer increases in factory orders and production. Steepest upturn in output charges since October 2013. Data collected Nov 11-25, 2024; released Dec 2, 2024. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/25dad0adf9ab4a0d9ffa8871efaecc52

2024-12.notes: Final Dec 2024 PMI = 56.4 (12-month low; 'Indian manufacturing ends strong 2024 with a soft note'). Data collected Dec 3-17, 2024; released Jan 2, 2025. Source: Business Standard citing S&P Global/HSBC final release.

2025-01.notes: Final Jan 2025 PMI = 57.7 (6-month high; rose from Dec 2024's 56.4). New orders grew at sharpest rate since Jul; export orders quickest in ~14 years. Flash estimate was 58.0; final revised slightly lower to 57.7. Data collected Jan 9-24, 2025; released Feb 3, 2025. Source: Business Standard citing S&P Global/HSBC final release.

2025-02.notes: Final Feb 2025 PMI = 56.3 ('down from 57.7 in January but still indicative of a further robust improvement'). 44th consecutive rise in new business intakes. Data collected Feb 6-24, 2025; released Mar 3, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/422ae3ee582a4421992040bd4709851e

2025-03.notes: Final Mar 2025 PMI = 58.1 (8-month high; rose from Feb's 56.3). Strong new orders drove output expansion. Data collected Mar 11-25, 2025; released Apr 2, 2025. Source: Business Standard citing S&P Global/HSBC final release.

2025-04.notes: Final Apr 2025 PMI = 58.2 — confirmed as prior-month reference in May 2025 PDF: 'Falling from 58.2 in April to 57.6 in May' (pdfplumber-verified). Data collected Apr 9-23, 2025; Apr final released May 2, 2025. Citation source (May 2025 PDF): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/6aa9940d1fbc42f596f354103e831393

2025-05.notes: Final May 2025 PMI = 57.6 (3-month low; 'PMI at three-month low amid softer growth of new orders and production'). India-Pakistan conflict weighed on growth. Series-record employment upturn. Cost inflation climbed to 6-month high. Data collected May 9-23, 2025; released Jun 2, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/6aa9940d1fbc42f596f354103e831393

2025-06.notes: Final Jun 2025 PMI = 58.4 (14-month high; 'rose to a 14-month high of 58.4 in June, up from 57.6 in May'). New export order growth surged near survey peak. Record employment upturn. Data collected Jun 9-24, 2025; released Jul 1, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/3cccb3ccb19040d3ac022eaa633b55aa

2025-07.notes: Final Jul 2025 PMI = 59.1 (16-month high; 'Rising from 58.4 in June to 59.1 in July'). Output and sales expand at quicker rates. Fastest overall sales in close to 5 years. Data collected Jul 9-25, 2025; released Aug 1, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/9806df5729e64068a9d3c6e17c8ee67b

2025-08.notes: Final Aug 2025 PMI = 59.3 (all-time survey high; 'Up from 59.1 in July to 59.3 in August'). Production increases at fastest pace since late-2020. Firms scale up input purchasing. Data collected Aug 7-22, 2025; released Sep 1, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/01fe3e9ca3a2447587f2e9651356a583

2025-09.notes: Final Sep 2025 PMI = 57.7 ('At 57.7 in September, down from 59.3 in August'). Growth moderated but remained above long-run average. Fastest rise in output charges in nearly 12 years. GST cuts boosted optimism. Data collected Sep 10-24, 2025; released Oct 1, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/b5951f0005ca45f0861f810f7e49501a

2025-10.notes: Final Oct 2025 PMI = 59.2 — confirmed as prior-month reference in Nov 2025 PDF: 'Falling from 59.2 in October' (pdfplumber-verified). Near 17-year high. Data collected Oct 2025; Oct final released Nov 3, 2025. Citation source (Nov 2025 PDF): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/abcf4297c5824fda82a1abd92968adfa

2025-11.notes: Final Nov 2025 PMI = 56.6 ('Registering 56.6 in November... Falling from 59.2 in October'). Weakest rises in sales and production since February. Job creation at 21-month low. Softer increases in input costs and output charges. Data collected Nov 11-24, 2025; released Dec 1, 2025. Source: S&P Global PMI press release PDF (pdfplumber-verified): https://www.pmi.spglobal.com/Public/Home/PressRelease/abcf4297c5824fda82a1abd92968adfa

2025-09.notes: JPC cumulative: Oct 2025 MER states Apr-Sep 2025 = 82.31 Mt (+12.4% YoY); minus Apr-Aug 2025 = 68.34 Mt → Sep 2025 = 13.97 Mt. YoY: Oct 2025 MER WSA para states 'September 2025 … a growth of 13.2% over September 2024.'

2025-10.notes: JPC cumulative: Nov 2025 MER states Apr-Oct 2025 = 96.08 Mt (+11.7% YoY); minus Apr-Sep 2025 = 82.31 Mt → Oct 2025 = 13.77 Mt. YoY: Nov 2025 MER WSA para states 'October 2025 … a growth of 5.9% over October 2024.' Marked deceleration from 13.2% in Sep; base-effect normalisation.

2025-11.notes: JPC cumulative: Dec 2025 MER states Apr-Nov 2025 = 109.49 Mt (+11.3% YoY); minus Apr-Oct 2025 = 96.08 Mt → Nov 2025 = 13.41 Mt. YoY: Dec 2025 MER WSA para states 'November 2025 … a growth of 10.8% over November 2024.'

2025-12.notes: JPC cumulative: Jan 2026 MER states Apr-Dec 2025 = 123.9 Mt (+10.8% YoY); minus Apr-Nov 2025 = 109.49 Mt → Dec 2025 = 14.41 Mt. YoY: Jan 2026 MER WSA para states 'December 2025 … a growth of 10.1% over December 2024.'

2026-01.notes: JPC cumulative: Feb 2026 MER states Apr-Jan 2026 = 138.8 Mt (+10.6% YoY); minus Apr-Dec 2025 = 123.9 Mt → Jan 2026 = 14.90 Mt. YoY: Feb 2026 MER WSA para states 'January 2026 … a growth of 10.5% over January 2025.' Jan 2026 at 14.90 Mt is the highest single month in the 20-month dataset.

2026-02.notes: JPC cumulative: Mar 2026 MER states Apr-Feb 2026 = 153.6 Mt (+11.2% YoY); minus Apr-Jan 2026 = 138.8 Mt → Feb 2026 = 14.80 Mt. YoY: Mar 2026 MER WSA para states 'February 2026 … a growth of 7.7% over February 2025.'

2026-03.notes: JPC cumulative: Apr 2026 MER states Apr-Mar 2026 = 168.4 Mt (+10.7% YoY); minus Apr-Feb 2026 = 153.6 Mt → Mar 2026 = 14.80 Mt. YoY: Apr 2026 MER WSA para states March 2026 rose 9.4% over March 2025 (WSA reported 15.32 Mt for Mar 2026 vs JPC 14.80 Mt; different scope/methodology).

2026-04.notes: Apr 2026 MER directly states: 'The production of Crude Steel for April 2026 is 14.09 million tonnes (Mt) (5.8% increase from the corresponding period of last year.)' Both production volume and YoY% are explicitly stated (Tier 1).

2026-05.notes: JPC cumulative: May 2026 MER states Apr-May 2026 = 28.0 Mt (+2.7% YoY over Apr-May 2025); minus Apr 2026 = 14.09 Mt → May 2026 = 13.91 Mt. YoY vs May 2025 (JPC 13.94 Mt) = 13.91/13.94 − 1 ≈ −0.2% (essentially flat). Apr-May cumulative +2.7% reflects April's +5.8% masking May's slight decline. May 2026 MER WSA para covers Apr 2026 (13.83 Mt WSA, +3.9%); May 2026 not individually stated.

2024-10.notes: SIAM pid=574 states PV sales = 3,93,238 units (+0.9% YoY over Oct 2023). Highest-ever October sales at that time; modest growth on a high base.

2024-11.notes: SIAM pid=575 states PV sales = 3,47,522 units (+4.1% YoY over Nov 2023).

2024-12.notes: SIAM pid=576: Dec 2024 = 3,14,934 units; +10.0% YoY over Dec 2023 (≈2,86,304 units). Strong year-end consumer demand; implied from SIAM Q3 FY25 press release.

2025-01.notes: SIAM pid=577 states PV sales = 3,99,386 units (+1.6% YoY over Jan 2024). Highest-ever January sales at that time.

2025-02.notes: SIAM pid=578 states PV sales = 3,77,689 units (+1.9% YoY over Feb 2024). Highest-ever February sales at that time.

2025-03.notes: Calculated: Mar 2025 (3,81,358, pid=579) / Mar 2024 (3,68,086, pid=562) − 1 = +3.6%. Mar 2024 confirmed from SIAM Q4 FY24 press release (pid=562).

2025-04.notes: SIAM pid=581 states PV sales = 3,48,847 units (+3.9% YoY over Apr 2024). Highest-ever April sales at that time; implies Apr 2024 ≈ 3,35,752 units.

2025-05.notes: SIAM pid=582: PV sales = 3,44,656 units; de-grew marginally by 0.8% YoY over May 2024 (≈3,47,435 units). 'Domestic sales held steady' per SIAM headline.

2025-06.notes: Calculated: Jun 2025 (3,12,849, pid=583) / Jun 2024 (3,37,757, pid=565) − 1 = -7.4%. Jun 2024 confirmed from SIAM Jun 2024 press release (pid=565). Q1 FY26 total was -1.4% vs Q1 FY25 (SIAM Q1 report pid=583); Jun bore the largest decline.

2025-07.notes: SIAM pid=584 states PV sales = 3,40,772 units (-0.2% YoY over Jul 2024). Marginal decline; subdued pre-festive consumer demand.

2025-08.notes: SIAM pid=590 states PV sales = 3,21,840 units (-8.8% YoY over Aug 2024). De-growth on strong Aug 2024 base (3,52,921 units).

2025-09.notes: SIAM pid=591: Sep 2025 = 3,72,458 units vs Sep 2024 = 3,56,752 units → +4.4% YoY. Festive season onset (Navratri) and GST reduction measures aided recovery.

2025-10.notes: Calculated: Oct 2025 (4,60,739, pid=592) / Oct 2024 (3,93,238, pid=574) − 1 = +17.2%. Festive surge (Navratri/Dussehra/Diwali); record October sales. SIAM states +17.2%.

2025-12.notes: Calculated: Dec 2025 (3,99,216, /595) / Dec 2024 (3,14,934, pid=576) − 1 = +26.8%. SIAM Q3 FY26 press release states '+27%' (rounded); calculated value = 26.8%.

2026-03.notes: Calculated: Mar 2026 (4,42,460, pid=605) / Mar 2025 (3,81,358, pid=579) − 1 = +16.0%. Tribune India cites SIAM: 'jumps 16% YoY'.

2026-04.notes: Calculated: Apr 2026 (4,37,312, pid=607) / Apr 2025 (3,48,847, pid=581) − 1 = +25.4%. SIAM states +25.4%; highest-ever April sales.

2026-06.notes: Jun 2026 SIAM PV YoY not yet available.
